sections.paragraph:
σής (since Pind., Fgm. 209 [OxfT=222 Sch./M.] Διὸς παῖς ὁ χρυσός. κεῖνον οὐ σὴς οὐδὲ κὶς δάπτει ‘gold is the child of Zeus: neither moth nor rust can consume it’; LXX), σητός (so Aristot., HA 5, 32; Menand. et al.; LXX; Philo, Abr. 11.—The class. gen. is σεός s. Kühner-Bl. I 510f), ὁ the moth, whose larvae eat clothing (Menand., Fgm.538, 5 Kö.=540, 5 Kock; Lucian, Ep. Sat. 1, 21 ἱμάτια ὑπὸ σητῶν διαβρωθέντα) Mt 6:19f; Lk 12:33. Being eaten by moths as a symbol of feebleness and destruction 1 Cl 39:5 (Job 4:19); B 6:2 (Is 50:9).—Worms, specific. wood-worms, seem to be meant (cp. Philo, Somn. 1, 77), since the σής is damaging sticks Hs 8, 1, 6f; 8, 4, 5; 8, 6, 4.—DELG. M-M. TW.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

OxfT OxfT = Oxford Text

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

class. class. = classical

gen. gen. = genitive

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 922
